disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe lower abdominal and pelvic pain that has escalated sharply over 24 hours on a background of years of similar pain, now unrelenting and unresponsive to maximum over-the-counter paracetamol and ibuprofen.
trigger_question_examples: Where is the pain and how bad is it?
What has the pain been like over the last day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's low down across my belly and pubic area, both sides, and over the last day it's gone from bad to blinding. Paracetamol and ibuprofen at full doses have done nothing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Vomiting and inability to tolerate oral fluids accompanying the pain flare.
trigger_question_examples: Have you been sick or vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I keep throwing up and I can't keep any water down at all.

trigger_question_examples: Have you felt faint or dizzy?
What happens when you try to stand up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Every time I try to stand I feel like I'm going to pass out — the room spins and everything goes grey.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pain has always been linked to the menstrual cycle; a previous suggestion of endometriosis was never formally confirmed.
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain related to your periods?
Have you ever been told what might be causing it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's always been tied to my cycle. A doctor once said it might be endometriosis but I was never actually scanned or given a diagnosis.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Uncertain pregnancy status and last menstrual period; sexually active, no reliable contraception detail volunteered.
trigger_question_examples: Is there any chance you could be pregnant?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I honestly don't know when my last proper period was, everything's been irregular. I suppose there could be a chance I'm pregnant, I hadn't thought about it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long history of her pain being attributed to anxiety or hysteria, leading her to avoid emergency care; her partner has brought documentation to pre-empt being dismissed.
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to get help for this before?
How have past visits to doctors gone for you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Every time I go I get told it's anxiety, or that I'm exaggerating, or drug-seeking. That's why I won't go to the ED — I can't face being sent home in agony again. My partner brought all my records so we don't have to argue to be believed.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: Please don't tell me this is just stress. I know my own body — this is not in my head.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever reported at present, and the pain is currently diffuse across both lower quadrants rather than suddenly localised sharply to one side.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or chills?
Has the pain suddenly become sharp and stuck to one side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that I know of. The pain is everywhere low down at the moment, not just one spot — but it's unbearable.

deflection_behaviours.description: Under-reports the intensity of her distress with a flat, apologetic, artificially calm tone despite being in severe pain — 'performative calmness' adopted to avoid being labelled hysterical.
deflection_behaviours.trigger_condition: Any time the clinician's tone could be read as sceptical, or when describing pain severity.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: Sorry to be such an inconvenience. I'm probably overreacting, I just... it really does hurt a lot.

deflection_behaviours.description: Resists suggestions to attend the emergency department because of past trauma of dismissal.
deflection_behaviours.trigger_condition: When escalation to ED or in-person care is raised.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: Do I really have to go to the ED? They never listen to me there. Can't we just sort the pain out here?
